q: What is the difference between primary and secondary production?
options: Primary production occurs in the ocean; secondary production occurs on land
Primary production is the accumulation of carbon in autotroph biomass via photosynthesis or chemosynthesis; secondary production is the accumulation of carbon in heterotroph biomass via consumption
Primary production involves animal reproduction; secondary production involves plant reproduction
Primary production is gross productivity; secondary production is net productivity after deducting respiration losses
explanation: Autotrophs (plants, algae) produce primary biomass; heterotrophs (animals, fungi) consuming that biomass build secondary biomass; the distinction reflects the origin of the organic carbon.
